explanation: The natural language requirement states that, whenever in performance review mode, explanations should always be detailed and contain all information. Since only the atomic proposition 'detailed' is given to capture the quality of the explanation (no explicit atomic proposition for 'contain all information'), we'll encode that, globally, when 'performanceReviewMode' holds, 'detailed' must also hold. That is, it should never be the case that the system is in performanceReviewMode and 'detailed' is false. This is captured with the LTL formula G(performanceReviewMode -> detailed). This ensures that at every point in time, if the system is in performance review mode, the explanation must be detailed, corresponding to the natural language requirement as far as atomic propositions allow. No numeric or data-level comparison is used, and only the provided atomic propositions are referenced.

The property expresses that whenever the system is in performance review mode, the explanation should be detailed. This is captured by the LTL formula G(performanceReviewMode -> detailed), which globally (at all times) requires that if 'performanceReviewMode' holds, then 'detailed' must also hold at the same time. Since only 'detailed' and 'performanceReviewMode' are given as atomic propositions, the requirement that the explanation 'contains all information' is subsumed under 'detailed' as per the atomic proposition description, and cannot be made more specific with the given atoms.

The natural language states that during performance reviews, any explanation should be both detailed and complete (contain all information). From the available atomic propositions, we capture that whenever the system is in 'performanceReviewMode', any explanation provided must be detailed. Since we do not have an explicit atomic proposition for 'contain all information', we use 'detailed' as a proxy for fulfilling the requirement of the explanation being complete and thorough. This is modeled by the LTL formula: G(performanceReviewMode -> detailed), which asserts that globally, whenever the system is in performance review mode, the 'detailed' atomic proposition must hold (i.e., explanations are detailed).

This LTL property requires that whenever the system is in performance review mode (performanceReviewMode is true), the explanation must always be detailed (detailed is true) at that moment. This condition holds globally, so at all times when the system is in performance review mode, the detailed property must hold. This matches the natural language requirement that during performance reviews, the explanation should be detailed and contain all information (assuming 'detailed' implies completeness of information), and is captured by the implication G (performanceReviewMode -> detailed). No numerical comparisons are used, and only provided atomic propositions are referenced.

The property aims to capture that whenever the system is in performance review mode, the explanation should always be detailed. To express that in LTL, we can state that it is always the case (globally) that if we are in performance review mode, then the "detailed" property must hold. This ensures that during all intervals when performanceReviewMode is true, detailed is also true. This does not check for "contain all information" as there is no atomic proposition for "all information," so we encode what is possible with the available atoms.

The natural language specifies that when the system is in performance review mode, the explanation should always be detailed. This means that globally, whenever 'performanceReviewMode' holds, 'detailed' must also hold. Thus, for every time point, if the system is in performance review mode, the explanation is detailed. This is best captured by the LTL formula G(performanceReviewMode -> detailed).

This LTL property asserts that whenever the system is operating in performance review mode (performanceReviewMode), the explanation must be detailed at that time. This captures the requirement that in performance reviews, the explanations should always be detailed—meaning, at every point where performanceReviewMode holds, detailed must also hold. This is well expressed by the formula G(performanceReviewMode -> detailed), which globally enforces this implication. No numerical comparisons are used, and only the given atomic propositions are referenced.

The property expresses that whenever the system is in performance review mode, the explanation must always be detailed. This is captured by the formula G(performanceReviewMode -> detailed). This ensures that in every state where 'performanceReviewMode' holds, 'detailed' must also hold, reflecting the natural language requirement that performance review explanations are always detailed. No numerical comparisons are required, and the atomic propositions map directly to the requirement.

The natural language statement requires that in performance reviews, all explanations must be detailed and contain all information. Since we have the atomic proposition 'detailed' (i.e., the explanation provided should be detailed), and 'performanceReviewMode' captures when the system is in a performance review, the LTL property should ensure that whenever the system is in performance review mode, the explanation is detailed. This can be best expressed as: globally, if performanceReviewMode holds, then detailed must also hold. This guarantees that in all states labeled as performance reviews, the explanation will be detailed. Since there is no atomic proposition for 'contains all information', we capture as much as possible from the available atomic propositions. Any previous properties that do not use the implication within the scope of 'G' may fail to guarantee that every performance review state enforces 'detailed'.

This LTL property states that whenever the system is in performance review mode (performanceReviewMode is true), globally (i.e., at all times during that mode), the explanation should be detailed. This matches the requirement that 'In performance reviews, the explanation should be detailed and contain all information'; since there is no atomic proposition for 'contain all information', we use 'detailed' as the best available representation. The formula G (performanceReviewMode -> detailed) expresses that always, whenever performanceReviewMode is true, detailed must also be true.
